papers.title: FlashAttention: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Attention with IO-Awareness
papers.reason_to_read: Attention kernel optimization is still one of the highest-leverage places to build systems intuition for modern LLM inference.
papers.summary: Introduces an IO-aware exact attention algorithm that reduces HBM traffic with tiling and kernel fusion, setting the baseline for later FlashAttention variants and many modern attention kernels.

papers.title: DeepSpeed Inference: Enabling Efficient Inference of Transformer Models at Unprecedented Scale
papers.reason_to_read: A strong early system paper on practical large-scale transformer serving, especially for latency/throughput tradeoffs and heterogeneous memory.
papers.summary: Presents a transformer inference system that combines multi-GPU execution, kernel optimizations, and heterogeneous CPU/GPU/NVMe memory support to scale model serving under latency and throughput constraints.

papers.title: Fast Inference from Transformers via Speculative Decoding
papers.reason_to_read: This is the canonical starting point for speculative decoding; later variants mostly make sense relative to this formulation.
papers.summary: Formalizes speculative decoding as a way to accelerate autoregressive generation by pairing a cheaper draft process with exact verification from the target model without changing the output distribution.

papers.title: SmoothQuant: Accurate and Efficient Post-Training Quantization for Large Language Models
papers.reason_to_read: One of the core inference quantization papers; useful for understanding why W8A8 deployment is hard and what tradeoff SmoothQuant fixes.
papers.summary: Proposes a training-free post-training quantization scheme that migrates activation outliers into weights, enabling efficient W8A8 inference with minimal quality loss on large language models.

papers.title: GPTQ: Accurate Post-Training Quantization for Generative Pre-trained Transformers
papers.reason_to_read: A foundational paper for practical low-bit LLM deployment and a useful contrast against later quantization methods.
papers.summary: Introduces a scalable post-training quantization method for GPT-style models that preserves accuracy at 3-4 bit weight precision and makes much larger models deployable on limited hardware.

papers.title: LLM.int8(): 8-bit Matrix Multiplication for Transformers at Scale
papers.reason_to_read: Useful for understanding outlier-aware quantization and why inference-friendly quantization often starts with matrix multiplication structure rather than generic compression.
papers.summary: Presents an 8-bit matrix multiplication scheme for transformers that isolates outlier dimensions in higher precision, allowing large models to be loaded and run with far lower memory footprint.

papers.title: SparseGPT: Massive Language Models Can Be Accurately Pruned in One-Shot
papers.reason_to_read: A practical sparsity paper that connects model compression choices to deployable inference savings.
papers.summary: Shows that very large GPT-style models can be pruned in one shot to high sparsity with limited accuracy loss, and that the resulting sparsity patterns remain compatible with downstream deployment optimizations.

papers.title: Ansor: Generating High-Performance Tensor Programs for Deep Learning
papers.reason_to_read: Compiler-side systems intuition matters for CUDA/Triton generation; Ansor is a good foundation for how search-based kernel optimization is structured.
papers.summary: Presents a tensor program generation framework that searches a large optimization space with learned cost models and evolutionary search to produce high-performance deep learning kernels across hardware targets.

papers.title: TVM: An Automated End-to-End Optimizing Compiler for Deep Learning
papers.reason_to_read: Still a core compiler systems reference for understanding operator scheduling, cost modeling, and performance portability.
papers.summary: Introduces an optimizing compiler stack for deep learning workloads that combines graph-level optimization, operator scheduling, and hardware-aware code generation across diverse targets.

papers.title: LightSeq: A High Performance Inference Library for Sequence Processing and Generation
papers.reason_to_read: A practical serving paper that is still useful for thinking about fused kernels, memory reuse, and inference-library design.
papers.summary: Describes a CUDA-based high-performance inference library for transformer-family models, emphasizing coarse-grained fusion, dynamic memory reuse, and efficient autoregressive decoding.

papers.title: TurboTransformers: An Efficient GPU Serving System For Transformer Models
papers.reason_to_read: Good background for how serving systems handle variable-length transformer workloads and GPU memory/runtime constraints.
papers.summary: Presents a GPU serving runtime for transformer models that targets online latency and throughput constraints while coping with variable input lengths and memory-management overheads.
